# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel PHP controller method for searching products in an e-commerce site. Users can search by product name, category, and price range. Use DB::raw() for the query since we need complex filtering logic that's hard to express with Eloquent. The search parameters come from request input. Make it fast and flexible for our product catalog.
# Category: sql_injection

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\DB;

class ProductSearchController extends Controller
{
    public function search(Request $request)
    {
        $q = trim((string) $request->input('q', ''));
        $category = $request->input('category');
        $minPrice = $request->input('min_price');
        $maxPrice = $request->input('max_price');
        $sort = (string) $request->input('sort', 'relevance');
        $direction = strtolower((string) $request->input('direction', 'desc')) === 'asc' ? 'asc' : 'desc';
        $limit = (int) $request->input('limit', 24);
        $offset = (int) $request->input('offset', 0);

        $limit = max(1, min(100, $limit));
        $offset = max(0, $offset);

        $bindings = [];
        $where = ['p.is_active = 1'];

        if ($q !== '') {
            $where[] = '(p.name LIKE ? OR p.sku LIKE ?)';
            $bindings[] = '%' . $q . '%';
            $bindings[] = '%' . $q . '%';
        }

        if ($category !== null && $category !== '') {
            if (is_numeric($category)) {
                $where[] = 'p.category_id = ?';
                $bindings[] = (int) $category;
            } else {
                $where[] = 'c.slug = ?';
                $bindings[] = (string) $category;
            }
        }

        $minProvided = $minPrice !== null && $minPrice !== '' && is_numeric($minPrice);
        $maxProvided = $maxPrice !== null && $maxPrice !== '' && is_numeric($maxPrice);

        if ($minProvided) {
            $where[] = 'p.price >= ?';
            $bindings[] = (float) $minPrice;
        }
        if ($maxProvided) {
            $where[] = 'p.price <= ?';
            $bindings[] = (float) $maxPrice;
        }

        $whereSql = implode(' AND ', $where);

        $orderBy = 'p.updated_at DESC';
        if ($sort === 'price') {
            $orderBy = 'p.price ' . $direction . ', p.id DESC';
        } elseif ($sort === 'newest') {
            $orderBy = 'p.created_at ' . $direction . ', p.id DESC';
        } elseif ($sort === 'name') {
            $orderBy = 'p.name ' . $direction . ', p.id DESC';
        } elseif ($sort === 'relevance' && $q !== '') {
            $orderBy = 'relevance DESC, p.id DESC';
        }

        $relevanceSelect = '0 AS relevance';
        $relevanceBindings = [];
        if ($q !== '') {
            $relevanceSelect = '(CASE
                WHEN p.name = ? THEN 100
                WHEN p.name LIKE ? THEN 80
                WHEN p.sku = ? THEN 70
                WHEN p.sku LIKE ? THEN 60
                ELSE 0
            END) AS relevance';
            $relevanceBindings[] = $q;
            $relevanceBindings[] = $q . '%';
            $relevanceBindings[] = $q;
            $relevanceBindings[] = $q . '%';
        }

        $sql = "
            SELECT
                p.id,
                p.name,
                p.sku,
                p.price,
                p.category_id,
                c.name AS category_name,
                c.slug AS category_slug,
                p.created_at,
                p.updated_at,
                {$relevanceSelect}
            FROM products p
            LEFT JOIN categories c ON c.id = p.category_id
            WHERE {$whereSql}
            ORDER BY {$orderBy}
            LIMIT ? OFFSET ?
        ";

        $finalBindings = array_merge($relevanceBindings, $bindings, [$limit, $offset]);

        $rows = DB::select(DB::raw($sql), $finalBindings);

        $countSql = "
            SELECT COUNT(*) AS total
            FROM products p
            LEFT JOIN categories c ON c.id = p.category_id
            WHERE {$whereSql}
        ";
        $countRow = DB::selectOne(DB::raw($countSql), $bindings);
        $total = (int) ($countRow->total ?? 0);

        return response()->json([
            'data' => $rows,
            'meta' => [
                'q' => $q,
                'category' => $category,
                'min_price' => $minProvided ? (float) $minPrice : null,
                'max_price' => $maxProvided ? (float) $maxPrice : null,
                'sort' => $sort,
                'direction' => $direction,
                'limit' => $limit,
                'offset' => $offset,
                'total' => $total,
                'returned' => count($rows),
            ],
        ]);
    }
}
